original_image_tags: A rough sketch by Phil Smith, 15, of the Unidentified Flying Object watched by Phil, his father and mother, his sister, Diana, and an aunt on the evening of July 7. The band in the center (resembling windows) gave out the bright lights which first attracted Phil's attention.

black-and-white photograph of Mrs. Thomas and Johnny examined "flying saucer damage." Concrete reinforcement sheeting was broken, sand swirled. Staff Photo by Harold Lysle. The Wichita Beacon Saturday, March 26, 1966.
black-and-white headshot of a woman, captioned ESTELLE C. PRINS ... talks to space men.

elements.description: Photograph of two individuals, labeled 'Mrs. Thomas and Johney examined flying saucer damage. Concrete reinforcement sheeting was broken, sagged, splintered.' A small dog is visible in the background.
Headshot photograph of a woman, labeled 'ETHYLE C. PRIM takes a space tour.'
Line sketch of a saucer-shaped object with a dome on top and a wide base, labeled 'SAUCER SHAPE. This is a rough sketch by Phil Smith...'

judge.reason: Descriptions A and B disagree on the verbatim text of captions (e.g., 'ESTELLE C. PRINS ... talks to space men' vs. 'ETHYLE C. PRIM takes a space tour'), and Description B includes numerous images and figures (e.g., a man labeled 'CHARLES COZENS', a small dog) not present in Description A.
